props.message:
THE SOURCEROOMS

A vast hall of bound scrolls. Each scroll is the source-text for one shared loom in the Loomhouse. Half the scrolls are missing pages.

What the realm was first told to be is written here. So is what it has become.

Carved into a reading-desk:

'The realm was written. The realm is being written. The realm will be written.

Walker — if you find a missing page, return it to the shelf. The Sourcerooms keep no other tax.'

root.speaker: Indexer Mor
root.text:
*he is up a ladder, sliding scrolls. He doesn't look down.*

Walker — careful with the floor scrolls. They look like rugs. They are not rugs.

What are you after?

what.speaker: Indexer Mor
what.text:
Source-text. The first writings of the realm. Each scroll says what one thing in the Wyrdmark was supposed to be.

A lot of them are wrong now. The realm is older than the writings. That's how it should be.

weird.speaker: Indexer Mor
weird.text:
*he pauses on the ladder.*

There's a scroll behind the back-shelf nobody pulls. The Indexer before me said it had a key in it. I never went looking — too tall, too tired, too busy.

If you're a walker who finishes things, walker — go find it. The realm might thank you.

bye.speaker: Indexer Mor
bye.text: Walk well. Mind the floor scrolls.

root.speaker: Scribe Tella
root.text:
*she's copying a scroll by hand. Her ink is the colour of deep water.*

Mor sends me to find the missing pages. I find some of them. The rest the realm is keeping for a reason.

Walk softly, walker. The shelves are listening.

props.open_message: A small dark key, tucked behind a back-shelf scroll nobody reads. The Indexer before Mor must have left it for the walker who would finish things. The realm thanks you.
